title: Regional Demand Forecasting + Inventory Optimization
bullet_1: End-to-end capstone connecting forecasting, inventory policy, and fulfillment strategy
bullet_2: Objective: improve planning accuracy, inventory efficiency, and operating decision quality
bullet_3: Final recommendation: XGBoost + policy-driven inventory + Carbon-first strategy
chart_mapping: Use project title visual or architecture summary
speaker_notes: Open with the business problem, not the code. This project is about making planning decisions more accurate, more explainable, and more actionable.

title: Business Context
bullet_1: Regional demand variability creates stock imbalance, cost leakage, and service risk
bullet_2: Forecasts alone are not enough; planners need inventory and fulfillment decisions tied to demand signals
bullet_3: The capstone was designed to connect prediction with operational action
speaker_notes: Frame the work as a decision system. The business value comes from linking demand insight to inventory and network choices.

title: Data Overview
bullet_1: Working scope covered 25 SKUs, 5 regions, and 6 categories
bullet_2: Daily time series rebuilt for ~70,625 SKU-region-day observations
bullet_3: Walk-forward holdout used 21 days to test real forecasting performance
chart_mapping: Use split summary or architecture flow summary if desired
speaker_notes: Highlight that the evaluation setup was time-aware and operationally realistic, not random split validation.

title: Forecasting Benchmark
bullet_1: Baseline methods included Naive, Seasonal Naive, MA7, and Drift
bullet_2: Best baseline was MA7 at 19.25% WMAPE
bullet_3: Baseline benchmarking established a credible performance floor before ML uplift
speaker_notes: Show that ML was earned, not assumed. Benchmarks matter because they prove value relative to simpler planning rules.

title: Machine Learning Improvement
bullet_1: XGBoost achieved 11.06% WMAPE versus Prophet at 11.16%
bullet_2: Accuracy improved by 42.5% versus the strongest baseline
chart_mapping: Use model comparison table and feature importance chart/table
speaker_notes: Explain why this matters operationally: better forecasts improve replenishment timing and reduce decision noise.

title: Inventory Policy Translation
bullet_1: Forecast outputs were converted into safety stock and reorder point logic across 125 SKU-region combinations
bullet_2: Safety stock value: $157,292.60; annual storage cost: $21,370.33
speaker_notes: This slide is the bridge from analytics to operations. Forecast accuracy becomes inventory policy and cash planning.

title: Strategy Comparison
bullet_1: Speed-first leads on fill rate and cost-to-serve
bullet_2: Carbon-first leads on lowest estimated carbon
bullet_3: In the current network proxy, Carbon-first also benefits from faster same-region fulfillment
speaker_notes: State the non-obvious insight clearly: sustainability and speed are aligned here because of routing structure, not because carbon is always faster.

title: Constrained Decision Engine
bullet_1: Recommendation is based on feasible trade-offs, not a single KPI
bullet_2: Decision logic evaluates service, cost, carbon proxy, and same-region routing under constraints
bullet_3: Default strategy: Carbon-first, with tactical override paths when service or cost thresholds are threatened
chart_mapping: Use Day 12C outputs if present; otherwise reference decision_rules_table.csv
speaker_notes: This is where the project becomes management-grade. The point is governed decision logic, not dashboard theater.

title: Financial Impact and ROI Story
bullet_1: 42.5% forecast improvement increased planning signal quality
bullet_2: Inventory layer quantified $157,292.60 safety stock and $780,850.78 replenishment needs
bullet_3: Business value comes from better stock positioning, lower avoidable urgency, and more confident strategy selection
chart_mapping: Use before_after_kpi_comparison.csv or financial impact summary if Day 12C exists
speaker_notes: Avoid claiming audited savings unless directly measured. Position ROI as decision-quality improvement with quantified operational exposure.

title: Recommendation
bullet_1: Use XGBoost as the demand planning model
bullet_2: Deploy policy-based safety stock and reorder points as the inventory control layer
bullet_3: Adopt Carbon-first as the default network strategy under current assumptions, with governed overrides
chart_mapping: Simple recommendation slide, no heavy chart required
speaker_notes: Make the recommendation crisp. One sentence per layer: forecast, inventory, strategy.

title: Risks and Limitations
bullet_1: Carbon values are synthetic comparative proxies, not formal ESG disclosure metrics
bullet_2: Performance is not uniform; worst region observed was West and worst category was Beauty
bullet_3: Scenario quality depends on the realism of warehouse, lead-time, and cost assumptions
chart_mapping: Use model risk register and assumptions register if desired
speaker_notes: Strong projects acknowledge limits cleanly. This builds credibility instead of weakening the story.

title: Next Steps
bullet_1: Productionize the forecasting and feature engineering refresh pipeline
bullet_2: Recalibrate inventory policy regularly using updated volatility and lead-time inputs
bullet_3: Stress-test network strategy under alternate cost, capacity, and routing assumptions
chart_mapping: Optional: architecture flow summary or roadmap graphic
speaker_notes: End on scale-up, governance, and business adoption. Show that this is implementation-ready thinking.
